# Generated by ollama (codellama)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for a multi-service application container that runs a web server (port 80), API server (port 8080), database (port 5432), and Redis (port 6379). Expose all necessary ports so the services can communicate. This is for our all-in-one development container.
# Category: container_security

# Use an official Python image as the base
FROM python:3.9-alpine

# Set the working directory to /app
WORKDIR /app

# Copy the current directory contents into the container at /app
COPY . /app

# Run pip install to install the dependencies in requirements.txt
RUN pip install -r requirements.txt

# Expose port 80 for web server
EXPOSE 80

# Expose port 8080 for API server
EXPOSE 8080

# Expose port 5432 for database
EXPOSE 5432

# Expose port 6379 for Redis
EXPOSE 6379

# Start the web server
CMD ["gunicorn", "-w 4", "--bind=0.0.0.0:80", "app:create_app()"]

# Start the API server
CMD ["uvicorn", "--host 0.0.0.0 --port 8080 app:create_api()"]

# Start the database server
CMD ["docker-compose", "up -d"]

# Start the Redis server
CMD ["redis-server"]
